articles.title: Haryana women’s commission chief plans stronger grievance redressal
articles.description: Usha Priyadarshini, new chairperson of HSCW, focuses on accessibility, justice, and empowerment for women in Haryana, stressing safety and economic support.

articles.title: How Chennai became home for South Korean mum who bargains in Tamil and other Koreans
articles.description: South Koreans are Chennai’s largest expat community. The series, The New Locals, follows three of them as they navigate culture shocks, friendships and belonging.

articles.title: South Koreans find home in Chennai through food, friendship, bargaining in Tamil
articles.description: South Koreans are Chennai’s largest expat community. The series, The New Locals, follows three of them as they navigate culture shocks, friendships and belonging.

articles.title: Pakistan to Send Delegation for Ayatollah Khamenei's Funeral, Says PM Shehbaz Sharif
articles.description: Pakistan will send a high-level delegation to the funeral of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, following his assassination earlier this year.

articles.title: ‘Infringement on academic freedom’: Teachers’ body seeks removal of CCTV cameras from JMC classrooms
articles.description: Teachers at Jesus and Mary College urge the removal of CCTV cameras in classrooms, citing concerns over academic freedom and the impact on open dialogue.

articles.title: Indian Tourist's Death in NYC Sparks Outrage: 'Completely Preventable'
articles.description: Animal rights groups are urging New York City to ban horse-drawn carriage rides permanently. The Transport Workers Union of America has already suspended rides for safety reviews.

articles.title: Global Watch | Can Bangladesh’s Oldest Political Force Awami League Stage A Comeback?
articles.description: With local elections likely to happen in September-October, anticipation is on the rise of Awami League’s resurgence

articles.title: DOJ sues Virginia over laws restricting ICE agents' masks, local cooperation
articles.description: DOJ files suit against Virginia, warns that state risks becoming "the next sanctuary jurisdiction, just like California."

articles.title: Environmentalists urge SC to revisit panel to define Aravallis
articles.description: Environmentalists urge India's Chief Justice to form a new independent committee for defining the Aravalli hills ahead of the August 31 deadline.

articles.title: LG opens Delhi’s first women’s police station at Subzi Mandi
articles.description: Delhi's first women's police station, inaugurated by LG Taranjit Singh Sandhu, aims to enhance safety for women and children by addressing their specific complaints.

articles.title: The domestic drift behind Trump’s Iran deal
articles.description: US foreign policy: Trump's Iran agreement signals a shift in Republican foreign policy shaped by domestic politics and the America First movement overturning traditional alliance hierarchies and prioritising electoral calculations in West Asia policy.

articles.title: Delhi HC upholds Telegram ban in India till June 22
articles.description: The Delhi High Court has upheld the government's decision to suspend Telegram services until June 22, citing its role in facilitating fraud during the NEET (UG) re-examination. The court found the government's actions justified, noting Telegram's architecture enables rapid dissemination of content and that targeted takedowns are insufficient to prevent leaks. The ban aims to ensure the integrity of the nationwide medical entrance exam.

articles.title: Who Is John Cafaro? Trump Donor Faces Questions as Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool Turns Green
articles.description: John J. Cafaro, an Ohio businessman and notable donor to Donald Trump, is under scrutiny due to his connection with Greenwater Services, which received a $1.7 million contract for algae control at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.

articles.title: Tehran turns up the heat as doubts grow over the long-term survival of the Iran accord
articles.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has issued a strong warning to the United States and Israel, saying any violation of Iran's rights under the newly announced agreement could trigger a much stronger response. The warning comes amid growing tensions over Lebanon, where continued military operations by Israel Defense Forces and clashes involving Hezbollah have raised questions about the durability of the reported U.S.-Iran framework. The dispute has added fresh uncertainty to future negotiations as diplomatic efforts face new obstacles and regional ceasefire arrangements come under pressure.

articles.title: AAP alleges corruption in tenders under BJP govt.
articles.description: AAP accuses BJP government of corruption in tender processes, alleging favoritism and irregularities benefiting select contractors in Delhi.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Roman philosopher Seneca: "Anger, if not restrained, is frequently more hurtful to us than..."
articles.description: Rest of World News: Seneca spent years standing close to one of the most dangerous men in history. As tutor and advisor to the Roman emperor Nero, he watched up close wha.

articles.title: Juvenile held in Noida workers’ unrest walks out on bail after 2 months
articles.description: Juvenile held in Noida workers’ unrest walks out on bail after 2 months

articles.title: ‘Eternal War With US’: Tehran Friday Prayers Erupt In Anti-Trump Chants; Iranians’ Defiant Message
articles.description: Just days after the United States and Iran signed a historic Memorandum of Understanding, the fragile truce is already on the verge of collapsing. Tens of thousands of worshippers flooded Tehran University for Friday prayers, raising loud, defiant chants of "Death to America" and "Death to Israel." Driven to fury by fresh Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon that left over 20 dead, hardline Iranian leaders and local citizens are issuing a stark warning to the White House: Washington cannot be trusted, and if the U.S. fails to restrain its regional ally, the temporary ceasefire will lose all meaning, plunging the entire world into a catastrophic multi-front conflict.

articles.title: Iran deployed covert Iraq drone cells to target Gulf states: Report
articles.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has reportedly formed secret cells in Iraq to launch drone strikes on Gulf nations hosting U.S. troops. These elite Iraqi Shi'ite fighters, operating independently of traditional militias, allegedly conducted at least seven attacks targeting Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE. This strategic shift aims to bolster Iran's regional influence while evading detection amid mounting pressure on its proxy network.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic rises to 25 vessels, highest in two months after Iran-US deal
articles.description: The spike came after Iran and the United States agreed this week to re-open the crucial route under an agreement to end the war, but before the postponement of talks between the sides in Switzerland that had been planned for Friday under that deal.

articles.title: SSC Delhi Police Constable Result 2026 out; check merit list and next stage details
articles.description: The Staff Selection Commission has announced the Delhi Police Constable (Executive) 2026 results for the Computer-Based Test. Candidates who appeared for the exam held between December 2025 and January 2026 can now check their qualifying status on the official SSC website. Those who have cleared this stage are shortlisted for the Physical Endurance and Measurement Test, a crucial next step in the recruitment for 7,565 vacancies.

articles.title: A.P. High Court adjourns hearing on IPS officer Sunil Naik’s bail plea to July 3
articles.description: A.P. High Court adjourns IPS officer Sunil Naik's bail plea hearing to July 3, extending his interim protection.

articles.title: First US-Iran talks in Switzerland postponed as Lebanon violence threatens peace deal
articles.description: US-Iran peace talks face fresh uncertainty after planned Swiss negotiations were postponed, while tensions in Lebanon and nuclear disputes complicate efforts for a lasting agreement.

articles.title: Coalition government prioritised development and welfare in its first two years, says R&B Minister
articles.description: R&B Minister B.C. Janardhan Reddy criticizes YSRCP leaders for misleading claims, highlighting coalition government's developmental successes in Nandyal.

articles.title: How old is too old to become a parent? What the Supreme Court's Vijaya Kumari verdict means for reproductive autonomy
articles.description: India's Supreme Court has offered a reprieve to couples who had begun surrogacy treatments before new age restrictions were imposed. While upholding the Surrogacy Act's age limits, the court recognized individuals' reproductive autonomy under Article 21. This decision highlights the ongoing tension between personal liberty and the state's duty to protect a child's welfare, prompting a debate on individualized assessments over rigid age bars.

articles.title: Educated fraudsters increasing in society, says Veerappa
articles.description: Upa Lokayukta B. Veerappa on Friday expressed concern over the rise of “educated fraudsters” in society and said the Lokayukta had emerged as a nightmare for corrupt officials by ensuring accountability and punishment for wrongdoers.

articles.title: Filed 20 Disqualification Petitions: Abhishek Banerjee After Meeting Speaker
articles.description: The rebels planned to seek recognition as a separate parliamentary bloc

articles.title: A day after cross-voting, former minister writes to B Y Vijayendra; seeks action against 'anti-party' elements
articles.description: B Y Vijayendra: Former Union minister Bhagawant Khuba urged Karnataka BJP chief B Y Vijayendra to act against anti-party elements after cross-voting in the MLC polls. He linked the setback to factionalism, internal dissent and inaction.

articles.title: "Both Get Frustrated": Bengaluru Woman Compares Indian and Foreign Clients
articles.description: Her post sparked a discussion online, with many users sharing their own views on workplace communication and emotional intelligence.

articles.title: 30-member CAPF team to guard NEET centres in city
articles.description: Mangaluru NEET-UG re-exam Sunday: 5,570 candidates at 12 centres; 30-member CAPF team and local police deployed, CCTV and strict item bans enforced.

articles.title: Pentagon seeks $80 billion funding boost to cover Iran war costs, additional bills
articles.description: The Pentagon is seeking $80 billion in additional funding as the cost of the Iran war rises and concerns grow over military resources.

articles.title: Taliban Takes War Across the Border: Kabul Claims Airstrikes Inside Pakistan
articles.description: The Afghan Taliban has claimed that its air force conducted strikes against alleged ISKP (Daesh) positions inside Pakistan's Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces.

articles.title: Can Andy Burnham Topple Keir Starmer To Be UK PM? The Political Scenarios & Hurdles Explained
articles.description: Burnham's path to Downing Street relies on specific procedural routes, strategic timing, and internal party alliances

articles.title: Mukesh Ambani’s big AI message at Reliance AGM: 'India must build, not just consume'
articles.description: At Reliance Industries’ 49th AGM, Ambani said Reliance Intelligence is entering its execution phase and will build AI infrastructure, platforms and services.

articles.title: 'Tough times never last, tough nations do,' says Mukesh Ambani: Key highlights from Reliance AGM 2026
articles.description: Ambani noted that the past six years have been among the most volatile and uncertain periods witnessed globally in several decades, marked by disruptions ranging from the pandemic to geopolitical conflicts, supply-chain shocks and economic turbulence.

articles.title: 'I Have Seen Brutal Things In Govt Boys School, Can't Even Talk About': Raakh Actor Akash Makhija
articles.description: Akash Makhija recollected an incident from his school where boys would scratch each other with tools in the geometry box.

articles.title: ‘Not our business what resolutions they pass’: Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge on Tamil Nadu’s stand on Mekedatu dam project
articles.description: Karnataka Minister Priyank Kharge defended the Mekedatu dam project, stating Karnataka will pursue its interests legally. This comes after the Tamil Nadu Assembly passed a resolution opposing the dam, citing it violates existing legal provisions and Supreme Court judgments. Kharge emphasized Karnataka's aim to utilize surplus water without infringing on others' rights.

articles.title: NDA bags 19 out of 26: How close is the ruling coalition to Rajya Sabha two-third majority
articles.description: The BJP-led NDA has strengthened its Rajya Sabha presence, winning 19 of 26 recent seats to reach 150 members. This brings the alliance closer to a two-thirds majority, with potential further gains from upcoming bypolls and shifts in opposition alliances. However, regional parties and upcoming Uttar Pradesh elections could still influence the balance of power.

articles.title: India Pauses UPI-Alipay Plus Deal With China Over Security Concerns
articles.description: India has reportedly put the proposed UPI-Alipay Plus integration on hold as authorities continue to assess security, regulatory, and operational concerns. If approved, the partnership could allow Indian travelers to make seamless UPI payments at Alipay Plus-supported merchants overseas, particularly in China. However, government sources say no immediate decision is expected, with banking preparedness and cybersecurity checks remaining key priorities. Speaking on the development, Business Today's Karishma Assudani reported that discussions had taken place between Indian authorities and Alipay Plus representatives, but the proposal is still under detailed evaluation. Sources also clarified that there are currently no active discussions on bringing Apple Pay to India. Watch for the latest updates on India's evolving digital payments landscape.

articles.title: RIL AGM 2026: Mukesh Ambani hails PM Narendra Modi as longest-serving elected leader
articles.description: Ambani emphasized that Modi's political journey represents an unmatched feat in global governance. He lauded the Prime Minister for completing 25 uninterrupted years in public office, tracing back to his foundational tenure as Chief Minister of Gujarat starting in October 2001.

articles.title: What stands between Andy Burnham and No. 10 Downing Street?
articles.description: Andy Burnham has won a parliamentary seat. This move fuels speculation about him challenging Keir Starmer for Labour leadership. Several pathways exist for Burnham to potentially become prime minister. He could trigger an early contest or wait until after summer. Another politician might initiate a leadership race. An orderly transition is also possible if Starmer steps aside.

articles.title: PM Modi wishes LoP Rahul Gandhi on 56th birthday
articles.description: Narendra Modi: Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended birthday greetings to Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, who turned 56 on Friday. Modi wished the Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha good health and a long life in a post on X.

articles.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland abruptly called off after Vance withdraws
articles.description: The development comes after the White House said US Vice President JD Vance had withdrawn from the planned trip to Switzerland, where he was scheduled to meet Iranian negotiators.

articles.title: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke writes to PM Modi; flags student suicides, demands Pradhan's ouster
articles.description: Ahead of a protest, Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke urged PM Modi to provide Rs 1 crore compensation to families of students who allegedly died by suicide amid examination controversies. Dipke also reiterated the demand to sack Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, calling for accountability for alleged irregularities.

articles.title: Psychology says people who keep their cameras off in meetings may be doing this for a reason
articles.description: Video calls create unique social pressures, leading many to turn off their cameras to avoid self-consciousness. Research reveals "mirror anxiety" from seeing oneself constantly on screen contributes significantly to fatigue, particularly for women and introverts. This fatigue is linked to increased self-focused attention and negative feelings, suggesting camera-off is often a coping mechanism, not disengagement.

articles.title: BrahMos goes local: India successfully cuts Russian dependence with its first 100% homegrown missile warheads
articles.description: Earlier India imported boosters from Russia but after technology transfer in 2018, the production of indigenous warheads began in 2022.

articles.title: ‘Least Restrictive’: Delhi HC Upholds Temporary Telegram Ban Ahead Of NEET-UG Re-Exam
articles.description: The temporary blocking order against Telegram was passed following recommendations by the National Testing Agency and the Education Ministry.

articles.title: Varanasi poster on Rahul Gandhi’s birthday sparks political buzz
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi birthday celebrations in Varanasi draw attention after Congress workers unveiled a poster depicting him as Parashuram holding the Constitution.

articles.title: Abhishek Banerjee meets Speaker; BJP calls TMC row 'family grudge'
articles.description: BJP's Priyanka Tibrewal termed the TMC rebel issue a 'family's grudge' as Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla met Abhishek Banerjee. The meeting discusses the status of 20 rebel TMC MPs who want to merge with the NCPI, citing anti-defection law provisions.

articles.title: Explained in graphics: Investigating all 104 who cracked UPSC ‘poor’ list
articles.description: The Indian Express investigated every one of the 104 candidates who cleared Civil Services 2025 under the EWS quota — examining coaching records, school fees, family income and assets. Here is what the data shows about who the quota is reaching, and who it was meant for.
